content.text: The local candidate list validates cleanly: `9,313` ZIP paths, all under `/volume1`, none in snapshots/recycle/metadata. I’m staging the list on `edgesynology1` and running a remote preview to confirm they still exist and estimate bytes before removal.

payload.name: shell_command
payload.arguments:
{"command":"$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss'
$localPayload = ".\outputs\delete_content_matched_zips_payload_$stamp.txt"
$localPreviewScript = ".\outputs\delete_content_matched_zips_preview_$stamp.sh"
$paths = @(Import-Csv .\outputs\edgesynology1_remaining_zip_content_matches_unique.csv | ForEach-Object { $_.zip_path -replace '^/mnt/edgesynology1/volume1','/volume1' } | Sort-Object -Unique)
[IO.File]::WriteAllText((Resolve-Path .\outputs).Path + "\delete_content_matched_zips_payload_$stamp.txt", (($paths -join "`n") + "`n"), [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
@'
set -euo pipefail
payload="$1"
count=0
missing=0
bad=0
bytes=0
while IFS= read -r p || [ -n "$p" ]; do
  p=${p%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$p" ] || continue
  count=$((count+1))
  case "$p" in /volume1/*.zip|/volume1/*.ZIP|/volume1/*.Zip|/volume1/*.zIp|/volume1/*.ziP|/volume1/*.ZIp|/volume1/*.ZiP|/volume1/*.zIP) ;;
    *) printf 'BAD\t%s\n' "$p"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;;
  esac
  case "$p" in *'/#snapshot/'*|*'/#recycle/'*|*'/@'*) printf 'BAD\t%s\n' "$p"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;; esac
  if [ ! -f "$p" ]; then
    printf 'MISSING\t%s\n' "$p"
    missing=$((missing+1))
    continue
  fi
  s=$(stat -c '%s' "$p" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  bytes=$((bytes+s))
done < "$payload"
printf 'PREVIEW\tcount=%s\tmissing=%s\tbad=%s\tbytes=%s\n' "$count" "$missing" "$bad" "$bytes"
'@.Replace('\"','"').Replace('\t',"`t").Replace('\n',"`n") | Set-Content -Encoding ascii $localPreviewScript
$remotePayload = "/tmp/delete_content_matched_zips_payload_$stamp.txt"
$remoteScript = "/tmp/delete_content_matched_zips_preview_$stamp.sh"
scp -O -q $localPayload "edgesynology1:$remotePayload"
scp -O -q $localPreviewScript "edgesynology1:$remoteScript"
$cmd = "bash '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; rc=`$?; rm -f '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; exit `$rc"
ssh -o BatchMode=yes edgesynology1 $cmd | Tee-Object -FilePath .\outputs\edgesynology1_delete_content_matched_zips_preview.txt","workdir":"D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2","timeout_ms":180000}

payload.name: shell_command
payload.arguments:
{"command":"$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss'
$localPayload = ".\outputs\delete_content_matched_zips_payload_$stamp.txt"
$localScript = ".\outputs\delete_content_matched_zips_execute_$stamp.sh"
$paths = @(Import-Csv .\outputs\edgesynology1_remaining_zip_content_matches_unique.csv | ForEach-Object { $_.zip_path -replace '^/mnt/edgesynology1/volume1','/volume1' } | Sort-Object -Unique)
[IO.File]::WriteAllText((Resolve-Path .\outputs).Path + "\delete_content_matched_zips_payload_$stamp.txt", (($paths -join "`n") + "`n"), [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
@'
set -euo pipefail
payload="$1"
count=0
missing=0
bad=0
bytes=0
while IFS= read -r p || [ -n "$p" ]; do
  p=${p%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$p" ] || continue
  count=$((count+1))
  case "$p" in /volume1/*.zip|/volume1/*.ZIP|/volume1/*.Zip|/volume1/*.zIp|/volume1/*.ziP|/volume1/*.ZIp|/volume1/*.ZiP|/volume1/*.zIP) ;;
    *) printf 'BAD\t%s\n' "$p"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;;
  esac
  case "$p" in *'/#snapshot/'*|*'/#recycle/'*|*'/@'*) printf 'BAD\t%s\n' "$p"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;; esac
  if [ ! -f "$p" ]; then
    printf 'MISSING\t%s\n' "$p"
    missing=$((missing+1))
    continue
  fi
  s=$(stat -c '%s' "$p" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  bytes=$((bytes+s))
done < "$payload"
if [ "$bad" -ne 0 ] || [ "$missing" -ne 0 ]; then
  printf 'ABORTED\tcount=%s\tmissing=%s\tbad=%s\tbytes=%s\n' "$count" "$missing" "$bad" "$bytes"
  exit 2
fi
deleted=0
failed=0
while IFS= read -r p || [ -n "$p" ]; do
  p=${p%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$p" ] || continue
  if rm -f -- "$p"; then
    deleted=$((deleted+1))
    printf 'DELETED\t%s\n' "$p"
  else
    failed=$((failed+1))
    printf 'FAILED\t%s\n' "$p"
  fi
done < "$payload"
printf 'SUMMARY\tvalidated=%s\tdeleted=%s\tfailed=%s\tbytes=%s\n' "$count" "$deleted" "$failed" "$bytes"
'@.Replace('\"','"').Replace('\t',"`t").Replace('\n',"`n") | Set-Content -Encoding ascii $localScript
$remotePayload = "/tmp/delete_content_matched_zips_payload_$stamp.txt"
$remoteScript = "/tmp/delete_content_matched_zips_execute_$stamp.sh"
scp -O -q $localPayload "edgesynology1:$remotePayload"
scp -O -q $localScript "edgesynology1:$remoteScript"
$cmd = "bash '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; rc=`$?; rm -f '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; exit `$rc"
ssh -o BatchMode=yes edgesynology1 $cmd | Tee-Object -FilePath .\outputs\edgesynology1_delete_content_matched_zips_execute_log.tsv","workdir":"D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2","timeout_ms":600000}

payload.name: shell_command
payload.arguments:
{"command":"$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$matches = Import-Csv .\outputs\edgesynology1_post_delete_zip_content_matches.tsv -Delimiter "`t" | Where-Object { $_.match_type -ne 'ZIP_READ_ERROR' }
$paths = @($matches | ForEach-Object { $_.zip_path -replace '^/mnt/edgesynology1/volume1','/volume1' } | Sort-Object -Unique)
$bad = @($paths | Where-Object { $_ -notlike '/volume1/*' -or $_ -notmatch '(?i)\.zip$' -or $_ -match '/(#snapshot|#recycle|@[^/]+)/' })
if ($bad.Count -gt 0) { throw "Refusing bad path: $($bad[0])" }
$stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss'
$localPayload = ".\outputs\delete_final_2_zips_payload_$stamp.txt"
[IO.File]::WriteAllText((Resolve-Path .\outputs).Path + "\delete_final_2_zips_payload_$stamp.txt", (($paths -join "`n") + "`n"), [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
$scriptPath = ".\outputs\delete_final_2_zips_$stamp.sh"
@'
set -euo pipefail
payload="$1"
count=0
missing=0
bad=0
bytes=0
while IFS= read -r p || [ -n "$p" ]; do
  p=${p%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$p" ] || continue
  count=$((count+1))
  case "$p" in /volume1/*.zip|/volume1/*.ZIP|/volume1/*.Zip|/volume1/*.zIp|/volume1/*.ziP|/volume1/*.ZIp|/volume1/*.ZiP|/volume1/*.zIP) ;;
    *) bad=$((bad+1)); printf 'BAD\t%s\n' "$p"; continue;;
  esac
  if [ ! -f "$p" ]; then missing=$((missing+1)); printf 'MISSING\t%s\n' "$p"; continue; fi
  s=$(stat -c '%s' "$p" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); bytes=$((bytes+s))
done < "$payload"
if [ "$bad" -ne 0 ] || [ "$missing" -ne 0 ]; then
  printf 'ABORTED\tcount=%s\tmissing=%s\tbad=%s\tbytes=%s\n' "$count" "$missing" "$bad" "$bytes"
  exit 2
fi
deleted=0
failed=0
while IFS= read -r p || [ -n "$p" ]; do
  p=${p%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$p" ] || continue
  if rm -f -- "$p"; then deleted=$((deleted+1)); printf 'DELETED\t%s\n' "$p"; else failed=$((failed+1)); printf 'FAILED\t%s\n' "$p"; fi
done < "$payload"
printf 'SUMMARY\tvalidated=%s\tdeleted=%s\tfailed=%s\tbytes=%s\n' "$count" "$deleted" "$failed" "$bytes"
'@.Replace('\"','"').Replace('\t',"`t").Replace('\n',"`n") | Set-Content -Encoding ascii $scriptPath
$remotePayload = "/tmp/delete_final_2_zips_payload_$stamp.txt"
$remoteScript = "/tmp/delete_final_2_zips_$stamp.sh"
scp -O -q $localPayload "edgesynology1:$remotePayload"
scp -O -q $scriptPath "edgesynology1:$remoteScript"
$cmd = "bash '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; rc=`$?; rm -f '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; exit `$rc"
ssh -o BatchMode=yes edgesynology1 $cmd | Tee-Object -FilePath .\outputs\edgesynology1_delete_final_2_content_matched_zips_log.tsv","workdir":"D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2","timeout_ms":60000}

Can you make a list of every file that exists in the styleguides share that also exists in the mac share? use edgesynology2 to do the check so we don't bog down edgesynology1. but we'll do the actual deletions on edgesynology1 when the time comes.

payload.message:
designers have been stupidly copying files (some of them 500MB or or more) from 'styleguides' into the folders in '\mac\Decor\Character Licensed' just so they remember what file names they used from the styleguides, which is incredibly wasteful. Can you make a list of every file that exists in the styleguides share that also exists in the mac share? use edgesynology2 to do the check so we don't bog down edgesynology1. but we'll do the actual deletions on edgesynology1 when the time comes. 
How would you match? by file name + hash? or file name + size? i think it's possible the file dates could be different even if it's the same content in the file

I’d match as `filename + size` first, then confirm with a content hash. Dates are noisy and I would ignore them. Hash-only would catch renamed copies too, but for your specific problem, `filename + SHA-256` is the cleanest evidence; using size first just keeps the work sane.

payload.name: apply_patch
payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: outputs/find_styleguide_copies_in_mac.py
+import csv
+import hashlib
+import os
+import sys
+from collections import defaultdict
+from pathlib import Path
+
+STYLE_ROOT = Path("/mnt/styleguides")
+MAC_ROOT = Path("/mnt/mac_character")
+CHUNK_SIZE = 8 * 1024 * 1024
+SKIP_DIRS = {"#snapshot", "#recycle"}
+
+
+def iter_files(root: Path):
+    for dirpath, dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root):
+        dirnames[:] = [
+            name for name in dirnames
+            if name not in SKIP_DIRS and not name.startswith("@")
+        ]
+        for filename in filenames:
+            path = Path(dirpath) / filename
+            try:
+                stat = path.stat()
+            except OSError:
+                continue
+            if not path.is_file():
+                continue
+            yield path, filename.casefold(), stat.st_size
+
+
+def sha256(path: Path) -> str:
+    h = hashlib.sha256()
+    with path.open("rb") as f:
+        while True:
+            chunk = f.read(CHUNK_SIZE)
+            if not chunk:
+                break
+            h.update(chunk)
+    return h.hexdigest()
+
+
+def main():
+    print("phase=scan_styleguides", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
+    style_by_name_size = defaultdict(list)
+    style_count = 0
+    for path, folded_name, size in iter_files(STYLE_ROOT):
+        style_count += 1
+        style_by_name_size[(folded_name, size)].append(path)
+        if style_count % 100000 == 0:
+            print(f"style_files={style_count}", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
+
+    print("phase=scan_mac_prefilter", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
+    candidates = []
+    mac_count = 0
+    for path, folded_name, size in iter_files(MAC_ROOT):
+        mac_count += 1
+        matches = style_by_name_size.get((folded_name, size))
+        if matches:
+            candidates.append((path, folded_name, size, matches))
+        if mac_count % 100000 == 0:
+            print(f"mac_files={mac_count} candidates={len(candidates)}", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
+
+    print(f"phase=hash_candidates candidates={len(candidates)}", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
+    style_hash_cache = {}
+    mac_hash_cache = {}
+    rows = []
+    candidate_pairs = 0
+
+    for i, (mac_path, folded_name, size, style_paths) in enumerate(candidates, start=1):
+        if i % 500 == 0:
+            print(f"hashed_candidate_groups={i} rows={len(rows)}", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
+        try:
+            mac_hash = mac_hash_cache.setdefault(mac_path, sha256(mac_path))
+        except OSError as exc:
+            rows.append({
+                "match": "MAC_HASH_ERROR",
+                "filename": mac_path.name,
+                "size_bytes": size,
+                "sha256": "",
+                "styleguide_path": "",
+                "mac_path": str(mac_path),
+                "error": repr(exc),
+            })
+            continue
+
+        for style_path in style_paths:
+            candidate_pairs += 1
+            try:
+                style_hash = style_hash_cache.setdefault(style_path, sha256(style_path))
+            except OSError as exc:
+                rows.append({
+                    "match": "STYLE_HASH_ERROR",
+                    "filename": mac_path.name,
+                    "size_bytes": size,
+                    "sha256": "",
+                    "styleguide_path": str(style_path),
+                    "mac_path": str(mac_path),
+                    "error": repr(exc),
+                })
+                continue
+            if style_hash == mac_hash:
+                rows.append({
+                    "match": "MATCH",
+                    "filename": mac_path.name,
+                    "size_bytes": size,
+                    "sha256": mac_hash,
+                    "styleguide_path": str(style_path),
+                    "mac_path": str(mac_path),
+                    "error": "",
+                })
+
+    writer = csv.DictWriter(
+        sys.stdout,
+        fieldnames=[
+            "match",
+            "filename",
+            "size_bytes",
+            "sha256",
+            "styleguide_path",
+            "mac_path",
+            "error",
+        ],
+        delimiter="\t",
+        lineterminator="\n",
+    )
+    writer.writeheader()
+    writer.writerows(rows)
+
+    match_rows = sum(1 for row in rows if row["match"] == "MATCH")
+    unique_mac = len({row["mac_path"] for row in rows if row["match"] == "MATCH"})
+    match_bytes = sum(
+        int(row["size_bytes"])
+        for row in {
+            row["mac_path"]: row
+            for row in rows
+            if row["match"] == "MATCH"
+        }.values()
+    )
+    print(
+        "SUMMARY"
+        f"\tstyle_files={style_count}"
+        f"\tmac_files={mac_count}"
+        f"\tcandidate_mac_files={len(candidates)}"
+        f"\tcandidate_pairs={candidate_pairs}"
+        f"\tmatch_rows={match_rows}"
+        f"\tunique_mac_matches={unique_mac}"
+        f"\tunique_match_bytes={match_bytes}",
+        file=sys.stderr,
+        flush=True,
+    )
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()
*** End Patch

$psi.RedirectStandardError = $true

}
$out = $outTask.GetAwaiter().GetResult()

$err

payload.name: shell_command
payload.arguments:
{"command":"$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$rows = Import-Csv .\outputs\edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_matches.tsv -Delimiter "`t"
$matches = @($rows | Where-Object { $_.match -eq 'MATCH' })
$errors = @($rows | Where-Object { $_.match -ne 'MATCH' })
$uniqueMac = @($matches | Group-Object mac_path | ForEach-Object {
  $first = $_.Group | Select-Object -First 1
  [pscustomobject]@{
    mac_path_edgesynology2_container = $_.Name
    mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path = ($_.Name -replace '^/mnt/mac_character','/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed')
    filename = $first.filename
    size_bytes = [int64]$first.size_bytes
    size_mb = [math]::Round(([int64]$first.size_bytes / 1MB), 2)
    sha256 = $first.sha256
    styleguide_match_count = $_.Count
    styleguide_paths = (($_.Group | ForEach-Object { $_.styleguide_path -replace '^/mnt/styleguides','/volume1/styleguides' }) -join ' | ')
  }
})
$uniqueMac | Sort-Object size_bytes -Descending | ConvertTo-Csv -NoTypeInformation | Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 .\outputs\edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_unique_mac_matches.csv
$matches | ForEach-Object {
  $_.styleguide_path = $_.styleguide_path -replace '^/mnt/styleguides','/volume1/styleguides'
  $_.mac_path = $_.mac_path -replace '^/mnt/mac_character','/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed'
  $_
} | ConvertTo-Csv -NoTypeInformation | Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 .\outputs\edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_all_match_pairs.csv
$byFolder = @($uniqueMac | Group-Object { ($_.mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path -replace '^/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed/','' -split '/')[0] } | Sort-Object { ($_.Group | Measure-Object size_bytes -Sum).Sum } -Descending | ForEach-Object {
  [pscustomobject]@{
    folder = $_.Name
    unique_mac_files = $_.Count
    bytes = ($_.Group | Measure-Object size_bytes -Sum).Sum
    gb = [math]::Round((($_.Group | Measure-Object size_bytes -Sum).Sum / 1GB), 2)
  }
})
$byFolder | ConvertTo-Csv -NoTypeInformation | Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 .\outputs\edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_summary_by_folder.csv
$byExt = @($uniqueMac | Group-Object { [IO.Path]::GetExtension($_.filename).ToLowerInvariant(); if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace([IO.Path]::GetExtension($_.filename))) { '[no extension]' } else { [IO.Path]::GetExtension($_.filename).ToLowerInvariant() } } | Sort-Object { ($_.Group | Measure-Object size_bytes -Sum).Sum } -Descending | ForEach-Object {
  [pscustomobject]@{
    extension = $_.Name
    unique_mac_files = $_.Count
    bytes = ($_.Group | Measure-Object size_bytes -Sum).Sum
    gb = [math]::Round((($_.Group | Measure-Object size_bytes -Sum).Sum / 1GB), 2)
  }
})
$byExt | ConvertTo-Csv -NoTypeInformation | Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 .\outputs\edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_summary_by_extension.csv
"match_pairs=$($matches.Count)"
"unique_mac_matches=$($uniqueMac.Count)"
"unique_bytes=$([int64](($uniqueMac | Measure-Object size_bytes -Sum).Sum))"
"unique_gb=$([math]::Round((($uniqueMac | Measure-Object size_bytes -Sum).Sum / 1GB),2))"
"errors=$($errors.Count)"
"top_folders:"
$byFolder | Select-Object -First 10 | Format-Table -AutoSize | Out-String
"top_extensions:"
$byExt | Select-Object -First 10 | Format-Table -AutoSize | Out-String
"largest:"
$uniqueMac | Sort-Object size_bytes -Descending | Select-Object -First 10 filename,size_mb,mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path | Format-Table -Wrap | Out-String","workdir":"D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2","timeout_ms":30000}

payload.message:
Done. I used `edgesynology2` only, with read-only mounts. No deletions.

Matching method: **same filename + same size as prefilter, then SHA-256 hash confirmation**. File dates were ignored.

Results:

```text
styleguides files scanned: 218,004
mac/Decor/Character Licensed files scanned: 386,511
same-name/same-size candidates: 7,424
confirmed duplicate match pairs: 10,476
unique mac files duplicated from styleguides: 6,876
space represented: 280,192,611,211 bytes
about 280.2 GB decimal / 261.0 GiB
```

Most of it is here:

```text
____New Structure   6,857 files   253.09 GiB
Puzzle                  1 file      7.33 GiB
Molded                 11 files     0.40 GiB
___OLD                  6 files     0.13 GiB
```

Biggest file types:

```text
.psd   1,494 files   178.03 GiB
.tif     531 files    30.26 GiB
.ai    3,552 files    26.48 GiB
.psb       4 files    18.47 GiB
.eps     868 files     5.67 GiB
```

Reports:

- [Unique mac files to consider deleting later](D:/repos/temp/edgesynology2/outputs/edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_unique_mac_matches.csv)
- [All styleguide/mac match pairs](D:/repos/temp/edgesynology2/outputs/edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_all_match_pairs.csv)
- [Summary by folder](D:/repos/temp/edgesynology2/outputs/edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_summary_by_folder.csv)
- [Summary by extension](D:/repos/temp/edgesynology2/outputs/edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_summary_by_extension.csv)

The unique report includes an `mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path` column using `/volume1/mac/...`, so it’s ready for deletion on `edgesynology1` when you want to do that.

payload.name: shell_command
payload.arguments:
{"command":"$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss'
$payload = ".\outputs\mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_payload_$stamp.tsv"
$script = ".\outputs\mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_preview_$stamp.sh"
$report = Import-Csv .\outputs\edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_unique_mac_matches.csv
$rows = @($report | Sort-Object mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path | ForEach-Object { "$($_.mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path)`t$($_.size_bytes)`t$($_.sha256)" })
[IO.File]::WriteAllText((Resolve-Path .\outputs).Path + "\mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_payload_$stamp.tsv", (($rows -join "`n") + "`n"), [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
@'
set -euo pipefail
payload="$1"
count=0
bad=0
missing=0
notfile=0
sizemismatch=0
conflict=0
already=0
preview=0
bytes=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r p expected_size expected_hash || [ -n "$p" ]; do
  p=${p%$'\r'}
  expected_size=${expected_size%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$p" ] || continue
  count=$((count+1))
  case "$p" in /volume1/mac/Decor/Character\ Licensed/*) ;;
    *) printf 'BAD_PATH\t%s\n' "$p"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;;
  esac
  case "$p" in *'/#snapshot/'*|*'/#recycle/'*|*'/@'*|*todelete) printf 'BAD_PATH\t%s\n' "$p"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;; esac
  dest="${p}todelete"
  if [ ! -e "$p" ]; then printf 'MISSING\t%s\n' "$p"; missing=$((missing+1)); continue; fi
  if [ ! -f "$p" ]; then printf 'NOT_FILE\t%s\n' "$p"; notfile=$((notfile+1)); continue; fi
  actual_size=$(stat -c '%s' "$p" 2>/dev/null || echo -1)
  if [ "$actual_size" != "$expected_size" ]; then printf 'SIZE_MISMATCH\t%s\texpected=%s\tactual=%s\n' "$p" "$expected_size" "$actual_size"; sizemismatch=$((sizemismatch+1)); continue; fi
  if [ -e "$dest" ]; then printf 'CONFLICT\t%s\t%s\n' "$p" "$dest"; conflict=$((conflict+1)); continue; fi
  bytes=$((bytes+actual_size))
  preview=$((preview+1))
  printf 'WOULD_RENAME\t%s\t%s\n' "$p" "$dest"
done < "$payload"
printf 'PREVIEW\tcount=%s\twould_rename=%s\tmissing=%s\tnot_file=%s\tsize_mismatch=%s\tconflict=%s\tbad=%s\tbytes=%s\n' "$count" "$preview" "$missing" "$notfile" "$sizemismatch" "$conflict" "$bad" "$bytes"
'@.Replace('\"','"').Replace('\t',"`t").Replace('\n',"`n") | Set-Content -Encoding ascii $script
$remotePayload = "/tmp/mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_payload_$stamp.tsv"
$remoteScript = "/tmp/mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_preview_$stamp.sh"
scp -O -q $payload "edgesynology1:$remotePayload"
scp -O -q $script "edgesynology1:$remoteScript"
$cmd = "bash '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; rc=`$?; rm -f '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; exit `$rc"
ssh -o BatchMode=yes edgesynology1 $cmd | Tee-Object -FilePath .\outputs\edgesynology1_mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_preview.tsv","workdir":"D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2","timeout_ms":180000}

payload.name: shell_command
payload.arguments:
{"command":"$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss'
$payload = ".\outputs\mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_payload_$stamp.tsv"
$script = ".\outputs\mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_execute_$stamp.sh"
$report = Import-Csv .\outputs\edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_unique_mac_matches.csv
$rows = @($report | Sort-Object mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path | ForEach-Object { "$($_.mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path)`t$($_.size_bytes)`t$($_.sha256)" })
[IO.File]::WriteAllText((Resolve-Path .\outputs).Path + "\mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_payload_$stamp.tsv", (($rows -join "`n") + "`n"), [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
@'
set -euo pipefail
payload="$1"
count=0
missing=0
ready=0
bad=0
notfile=0
sizemismatch=0
conflict=0
bytes=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r p expected_size expected_hash || [ -n "$p" ]; do
  p=${p%$'\r'}
  expected_size=${expected_size%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$p" ] || continue
  count=$((count+1))
  case "$p" in /volume1/mac/Decor/Character\ Licensed/*) ;;
    *) printf 'BAD_PATH\t%s\n' "$p"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;;
  esac
  case "$p" in *'/#snapshot/'*|*'/#recycle/'*|*'/@'*|*todelete) printf 'BAD_PATH\t%s\n' "$p"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;; esac
  dest="${p}todelete"
  if [ ! -e "$p" ]; then missing=$((missing+1)); continue; fi
  if [ ! -f "$p" ]; then printf 'NOT_FILE\t%s\n' "$p"; notfile=$((notfile+1)); continue; fi
  actual_size=$(stat -c '%s' "$p" 2>/dev/null || echo -1)
  if [ "$actual_size" != "$expected_size" ]; then printf 'SIZE_MISMATCH\t%s\texpected=%s\tactual=%s\n' "$p" "$expected_size" "$actual_size"; sizemismatch=$((sizemismatch+1)); continue; fi
  if [ -e "$dest" ]; then printf 'CONFLICT\t%s\t%s\n' "$p" "$dest"; conflict=$((conflict+1)); continue; fi
  bytes=$((bytes+actual_size))
  ready=$((ready+1))
done < "$payload"
if [ "$bad" -ne 0 ] || [ "$notfile" -ne 0 ] || [ "$sizemismatch" -ne 0 ] || [ "$conflict" -ne 0 ]; then
  printf 'ABORTED\tcount=%s\tready=%s\tmissing=%s\tnot_file=%s\tsize_mismatch=%s\tconflict=%s\tbad=%s\tbytes=%s\n' "$count" "$ready" "$missing" "$notfile" "$sizemismatch" "$conflict" "$bad" "$bytes"
  exit 2
fi
renamed=0
failed=0
skipped_missing=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r p expected_size expected_hash || [ -n "$p" ]; do
  p=${p%$'\r'}
  expected_size=${expected_size%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$p" ] || continue
  dest="${p}todelete"
  if [ ! -e "$p" ]; then skipped_missing=$((skipped_missing+1)); printf 'SKIPPED_MISSING\t%s\n' "$p"; continue; fi
  if mv -- "$p" "$dest"; then
    renamed=$((renamed+1))
    printf 'RENAMED\t%s\t%s\n' "$p" "$dest"
  else
    failed=$((failed+1))
    printf 'FAILED\t%s\t%s\n' "$p" "$dest"
  fi
done < "$payload"
printf 'SUMMARY\tvalidated=%s\tready=%s\trenamed=%s\tfailed=%s\tskipped_missing=%s\tbytes=%s\n' "$count" "$ready" "$renamed" "$failed" "$skipped_missing" "$bytes"
'@.Replace('\"','"').Replace('\t',"`t").Replace('\n',"`n") | Set-Content -Encoding ascii $script
$remotePayload = "/tmp/mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_payload_$stamp.tsv"
$remoteScript = "/tmp/mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_execute_$stamp.sh"
scp -O -q $payload "edgesynology1:$remotePayload"
scp -O -q $script "edgesynology1:$remoteScript"
$cmd = "bash '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; rc=`$?; rm -f '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; exit `$rc"
ssh -o BatchMode=yes edgesynology1 $cmd | Tee-Object -FilePath .\outputs\edgesynology1_mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_execute_log.tsv","workdir":"D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2","timeout_ms":300000}

{"command":"$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss'
$renamed = Import-Csv .\outputs\edgesynology1_mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_execute_log.tsv -Delimiter "`t" -Header status,source,dest | Where-Object { $_.status -eq 'RENAMED' }
$payload = ".\outputs\verify_mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_payload_$stamp.tsv"
$rows = @($renamed | ForEach-Object { "$($_.source)`t$($_.dest)" })
[IO.File]::WriteAllText((Resolve-Path .\outputs).Path + "\verify_mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_payload_$stamp.tsv", (($rows -join "`n") + "`n"), [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
$script = ".\outputs\verify_mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_$stamp.sh"
@'
set -euo pipefail
payload="$1"
count=0
ok=0
source_still_exists=0
dest_missing=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r src dest || [ -n "$src" ]; do
  src=${src%$'\r'}
  dest=${dest%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$src" ] || continue
  count=$((count+1))
  s=0; d=0
  if [ -e "$src" ]; then s=1; source_still_exists=$((source_still_exists+1)); printf 'SOURCE_STILL_EXISTS\t%s\n' "$src"; fi
  if [ ! -f "$dest" ]; then d=1; dest_missing=$((dest_missing+1)); printf 'DEST_MISSING\t%s\n' "$dest"; fi
  if [ "$s" -eq 0 ] && [ "$d" -eq 0 ]; then ok=$((ok+1)); fi
done < "$payload"
printf 'VERIFY\tcount=%s\tok=%s\tsource_still_exists=%s\tdest_missing=%s\n' "$count" "$ok" "$source_still_exists" "$dest_missing"
'@.Replace('\"','"').Replace('\t',"`t").Replace('\n',"`n") | Set-Content -Encoding ascii $script
$remotePayload = "/tmp/verify_mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_payload_$stamp.tsv"
$remoteScript = "/tmp/verify_mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_$stamp.sh"
scp -O -q $payload "edgesynology1:$remotePayload"
scp -O -q $script "edgesynology1:$remoteScript"
$cmd = "bash '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; rc=`$?; rm -f '$remoteScript' '$remotePayload'; exit `$rc"
ssh -o BatchMode=yes edgesynology1 $cmd | Tee-Object -FilePath .\outputs\edgesynology1_mark_styleguide_dupes_todelete_verify.tsv","workdir":"D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2","timeout_ms":180000}

<permissions instructions>
Filesystem sandboxing defines which files can be read or written. `sandbox_mode` is `workspace-write`: The sandbox permits reading files, and editing files in `cwd` and `writable_roots`. Editing files in other directories requires approval. Network access is restricted.
# Escalation Requests

Commands are run outside the sandbox if they are approved by the user, or match an existing rule that allows it to run unrestricted. The command string is split into independent command segments at shell control operators, including but not limited to:

- Pipes: |
- Logical operators: &&, ||
- Command separators: ;
- Subshell boundaries: (...), $(...)

Each resulting segment is evaluated independently for sandbox restrictions and approval requirements.

Example:

git pull | tee output.txt

This is treated as two command segments:

["git", "pull"]

["tee", "output.txt"]

Commands that use more advanced shell features like redirection (>, >>, <), substitutions ($(...), ...), environment variables (FOO=bar), or wildcard patterns (*, ?) will not be evaluated against rules, to limit the scope of what an approved rule allows.

## How to request escalation

IMPORTANT: To request approval to execute a command that will require escalated privileges:

- Provide the `sandbox_permissions` parameter with the value `"require_escalated"`
- Include a short question asking the user if they want to allow the action in `justification` parameter. e.g. "Do you want to download and install dependencies for this project?"
- Optionally suggest a `prefix_rule` - this will be shown to the user with an option to persist the rule approval for future sessions.

If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with "require_escalated". ALWAYS proceed to use the `justification` parameter - do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.

## When to request escalation

While commands are running inside the sandbox, here are some scenarios that will require escalation outside the sandbox:

- You need to run a command that writes to a directory that requires it (e.g. running tests that write to /var)
- You need to run a GUI app (e.g., open/xdg-open/osascript) to open browsers or files.
- If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with `require_escalated`. ALWAYS proceed to use the `sandbox_permissions` and `justification` parameters. do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.
- You are about to take a potentially destructive action such as an `rm` or `git reset` that the user did not explicitly ask for.
- Be judicious with escalating, but if completing the user's request requires it, you should do so - don't try and circumvent approvals by using other tools.

## prefix_rule guidance

When choosing a `prefix_rule`, request one that will allow you to fulfill similar requests from the user in the future without re-requesting escalation. It should be categorical and reasonably scoped to similar capabilities. You should rarely pass the entire command into `prefix_rule`.

### Banned prefix_rules 
Avoid requesting overly broad prefixes that the user would be ill-advised to approve. For example, do not request ["python3"], ["python", "-"], or other similar prefixes that would allow arbitrary scripting.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule argument for destructive commands like rm.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule if your command uses a heredoc or herestring. 

### Examples
Good examples of prefixes:
- ["npm", "run", "dev"]
- ["gh", "pr", "check"]
- ["cargo", "test"]


## Approved command prefixes
The following prefix rules have already been approved: - ["git", "add"]
- ["git", "push"]
- ["git", "clone"]
- ["git", "commit"]
- ["git", "remote", "-v"]
- ["C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe", "-Command", "gh pr view 40 --json number,title,url,headRefName,baseRefName,commits"]
- ["C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe", "-Command", "gh pr view 117 --json number,title,url,headRefName,baseRefName,commits"]
- ["C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe", "-Command", "gh pr list --head sandbox-albert --base develop --state open --json number,title,url"]
 The writable roots are `C:\tmp`, `D:\repos\dflow`, `D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2`.

content.text: can we create shortcuts / symlinks (whatever would work in windows and mac) from the duplicated files in 'mac/Decor/Character Licensed' to the original's location in /styleguides/ ?

payload.message: can we create shortcuts / symlinks (whatever would work in windows and mac) from the duplicated files in 'mac/Decor/Character Licensed' to the original's location in /styleguides/ ?

Short answer: **not in a clean, reliable Windows + Mac way**.

Best options:

1. **Unix symlinks on Synology**
   - Works well on Synology/Linux.
   - May work on Mac over SMB depending on SMB settings.
   - Often does **not** work reliably on Windows over SMB, especially when linking across shares like `/mac` -> `/styleguides`.
   - Cross-share symlinks are usually treated as “wide links,” which SMB often blocks for security.

2. **Windows `.lnk` shortcuts**
   - Work on Windows.
   - Mac users just see shortcut files, not usable links.

3. **Mac Finder aliases**
   - Work on Mac.
   - Windows users just see alias files, not usable links.

4. **Hard links**
   - Would work for both Windows and Mac because they look like real files.
   - But they are dangerous here: editing the “copy” would edit the original styleguide file too.
   - Also only works if both files are on the same filesystem, which they probably are, but I would not use this unless styleguides are locked read-only.

My recommendation: **don’t replace these with symlinks/shortcuts.** The better workflow is:

- Rename/delete duplicate copied assets from `mac/Decor/Character Licensed`.
- Generate a reference report next to the project folder, like `_styleguide_files_used.tsv` or `_styleguide_links.html`, listing:
  - copied filename
  - original styleguide path
  - size/hash
  - maybe a `smb://...` path for Mac and `\\server\styleguides\...` path for Windows

That gives designers the “what file did I use?” memory aid without wasting hundreds of GB or relying on fragile cross-platform link behavior.

content.text: there's no read-only version of Hard Links?

payload.message: there's no read-only version of Hard Links?

No. A hard link is just another directory entry pointing to the exact same file inode/data. There is no “read-only hard link” that protects the original while allowing the linked copy to behave differently.

If someone edits either path, they edit the same underlying file.

Permissions are also effectively tied to the same underlying file. You can make the file read-only, but then **both** paths are read-only. You can’t make only the hard-linked copy read-only while the original stays writable, or vice versa.

So hard links are only safe here if the styleguide originals are meant to be immutable/read-only for designers. Otherwise they’re risky because a project-folder “copy” could accidentally modify the original styleguide asset.

content.text: styleguide originals are meant to be immutable/read-only for designers

payload.message: styleguide originals are meant to be immutable/read-only for designers

Then yes, **hard links become a much better option**.

If `/volume1/styleguides` and `/volume1/mac` are on the same filesystem/volume, a hard link would let this exist:

```text
/volume1/styleguides/.../SomeAsset.psd
/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed/.../SomeAsset.psd
```

Both paths would look like normal files to Windows and Mac users over SMB, but they would point to the same underlying data. No shortcut weirdness, no symlink compatibility problems, no duplicate storage.

Important details:

```text
Hard links work for files, not folders.
They only work within the same filesystem/volume.
Deleting the mac-side hard link does not delete the styleguide file if the styleguide path still exists.
Editing either path edits the same underlying file.
Permissions are shared because it is the same file.
```

Since designers should not be editing styleguide originals anyway, this could be a solid cleanup pattern:

1. Confirm `/volume1/styleguides` and `/volume1/mac` are same filesystem.
2. Confirm target duplicate is exact same content as styleguide original.
3. Rename/remove the duplicate copy.
4. Create a hard link at the old mac path pointing to the styleguide original.
5. Verify link count/inode match.

The key question is whether you want these mac project folders to still “contain” the files visually. If yes, hard links are probably the best cross-platform option.

content.text:
Then yes, **hard links become a much better option**.

If `/volume1/styleguides` and `/volume1/mac` are on the same filesystem/volume, a hard link would let this exist:

```text
/volume1/styleguides/.../SomeAsset.psd
/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed/.../SomeAsset.psd
```

Both paths would look like normal files to Windows and Mac users over SMB, but they would point to the same underlying data. No shortcut weirdness, no symlink compatibility problems, no duplicate storage.

Important details:

```text
Hard links work for files, not folders.
They only work within the same filesystem/volume.
Deleting the mac-side hard link does not delete the styleguide file if the styleguide path still exists.
Editing either path edits the same underlying file.
Permissions are shared because it is the same file.
```

Since designers should not be editing styleguide originals anyway, this could be a solid cleanup pattern:

1. Confirm `/volume1/styleguides` and `/volume1/mac` are same filesystem.
2. Confirm target duplicate is exact same content as styleguide original.
3. Rename/remove the duplicate copy.
4. Create a hard link at the old mac path pointing to the styleguide original.
5. Verify link count/inode match.

The key question is whether you want these mac project folders to still “contain” the files visually. If yes, hard links are probably the best cross-platform option.

payload.message:
Yes to both.

1. **How to make hard-linked files read-only automatically**

A hard link has no independent permissions. The read-only setting must be applied to the underlying file itself, which means it affects both paths:

```text
/styleguides/original.psd
/mac/Decor/Character Licensed/.../original.psd
```

If they are hard links, they are the same file. So the safe automation is:

```bash
chmod a-w "/volume1/styleguides/path/to/file.psd"
ln "/volume1/styleguides/path/to/file.psd" "/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed/.../file.psd"
```

or create the hard link first, then:

```bash
chmod a-w "/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed/.../file.psd"
```

Same result either way.

Important nuance: it should be **filesystem-level read-only**, not just “the styleguides SMB share is read-only.” If designers access the same file through the writable `mac` share, share-level read-only on `styleguides` may not protect it. The file’s own permissions/ACL need to prevent writes.

2. **Yes, we can test on one folder**

Safe test plan:

```text
1. Pick one small folder in mac/Decor/Character Licensed with files already marked .todelete.
2. For each .todelete file, find the confirmed styleguides original from our report.
3. Create a hard link at the original mac filename.
4. Make the underlying file read-only.
5. Verify:
   - mac path and styleguides path have same inode
   - link count increased
   - file is not writable
   - Windows/Mac should see it as a normal file
6. Leave the .todelete copy in place during the test, so rollback is easy.
```

For example, after test:

```text
SomeAsset.psd           <- hard link to /styleguides original
SomeAsset.psdtodelete   <- old duplicate copy, kept temporarily
```

Rollback is easy:

```bash
rm "SomeAsset.psd"
mv "SomeAsset.psdtodelete" "SomeAsset.psd"
```

I’d test with a folder containing a few small `.ai/.png/.eps` files first, not a 7 GB monster.

payload.name: exec_command
payload.arguments:
{"cmd":"$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$folder = '/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed/____New Structure/Concept Approved Designs/DESKTOP/CDD_Figural ceramic pencil cup/Disney/CDD58DYWP01/For Factory'
$rows = Import-Csv .\outputs\edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_unique_mac_matches.csv |
  Where-Object { (Split-Path $_.mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path -Parent).Replace('\\','/') -eq $folder }
if (@($rows).Count -ne 5) { throw "Expected 5 rows, found $(@($rows).Count)" }
$stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss'
$payload = ".\outputs\hardlink_test_payload_$stamp.tsv"
$script = ".\outputs\hardlink_test_$stamp.sh"
$payloadLines = @($rows | Sort-Object filename | ForEach-Object {
  $style = ($_.styleguide_paths -split ' \| ')[0]
  "$($_.mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path)`t$style`t$($_.size_bytes)"
})
[IO.File]::WriteAllText((Resolve-Path .\outputs).Path + "\hardlink_test_payload_$stamp.tsv", (($payloadLines -join "`n") + "`n"), [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
@'
set -euo pipefail
payload="$1"
ready=0
bad=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r mac style expected_size || [ -n "$mac" ]; do
  mac=${mac%$'\r'}; style=${style%$'\r'}; expected_size=${expected_size%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$mac" ] || continue
  old="${mac}todelete"
  case "$mac" in /volume1/mac/Decor/Character\ Licensed/*) ;;
    *) printf 'BAD_MAC_PATH\t%s\n' "$mac"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;;
  esac
  case "$style" in /volume1/styleguides/*) ;;
    *) printf 'BAD_STYLE_PATH\t%s\n' "$style"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;;
  esac
  if [ -e "$mac" ]; then printf 'MAC_EXISTS\t%s\n' "$mac"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue; fi
  if [ ! -f "$old" ]; then printf 'OLD_MISSING\t%s\n' "$old"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue; fi
  if [ ! -f "$style" ]; then printf 'STYLE_MISSING\t%s\n' "$style"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue; fi
  style_size=$(stat -c '%s' "$style")
  old_size=$(stat -c '%s' "$old")
  if [ "$style_size" != "$expected_size" ] || [ "$old_size" != "$expected_size" ]; then
    printf 'SIZE_MISMATCH\t%s\texpected=%s\tstyle=%s\told=%s\n' "$mac" "$expected_size" "$style_size" "$old_size"
    bad=$((bad+1)); continue
  fi
  style_dev=$(stat -c '%d' "$style")
  parent_dev=$(stat -c '%d' "$(dirname "$mac")")
  if [ "$style_dev" != "$parent_dev" ]; then
    printf 'DIFFERENT_FILESYSTEM\t%s\t%s\n' "$style" "$mac"
    bad=$((bad+1)); continue
  fi
  ready=$((ready+1))
done < "$payload"
printf 'PRECHECK\tready=%s\tbad=%s\n' "$ready" "$bad"
[ "$bad" -eq 0 ] || exit 2
created=0
chmodded=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r mac style expected_size || [ -n "$mac" ]; do
  mac=${mac%$'\r'}; style=${style%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$mac" ] || continue
  ln "$style" "$mac"
  created=$((created+1))
  chmod a-w "$style"
  chmodded=$((chmodded+1))
  style_stat=$(stat -c '%d:%i:%h:%A:%a' "$style")
  mac_stat=$(stat -c '%d:%i:%h:%A:%a' "$mac")
  old_stat=$(stat -c '%d:%i:%h:%A:%a' "${mac}todelete")
  printf 'LINKED\t%s\t%s\tstyle_stat=%s\tmac_stat=%s\told_stat=%s\n' "$style" "$mac" "$style_stat" "$mac_stat" "$old_stat"
done < "$payload"
printf 'SUMMARY\tcreated=%s\tchmodded=%s\n' "$created" "$chmodded"
'@.Replace('\"','"').Replace('\t',"`t").Replace('\n',"`n") | Set-Content -Encoding ascii $script
"payload=$payload"
"script=$script"","workdir":"D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":12000}

payload.arguments:
{"cmd":"$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$folder = '/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed/____New Structure/Concept Approved Designs/DESKTOP/CDD_Figural ceramic pencil cup/Disney/CDD58DYWP01/For Factory'
$rows = @(Import-Csv .\outputs\edgesynology2_styleguides_in_mac_character_unique_mac_matches.csv |
  Where-Object { $_.mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path.StartsWith($folder + '/') })
if ($rows.Count -ne 5) { throw "Expected 5 rows, found $($rows.Count)" }
$stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss'
$payload = ".\outputs\hardlink_test_payload_$stamp.tsv"
$script = ".\outputs\hardlink_test_$stamp.sh"
$payloadLines = @($rows | Sort-Object filename | ForEach-Object {
  $style = ($_.styleguide_paths -split ' \| ')[0]
  "$($_.mac_path_edgesynology1_delete_path)`t$style`t$($_.size_bytes)"
})
[IO.File]::WriteAllText((Resolve-Path .\outputs).Path + "\hardlink_test_payload_$stamp.tsv", (($payloadLines -join "`n") + "`n"), [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
@'
set -euo pipefail
payload="$1"
ready=0
bad=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r mac style expected_size || [ -n "$mac" ]; do
  mac=${mac%$'\r'}; style=${style%$'\r'}; expected_size=${expected_size%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$mac" ] || continue
  old="${mac}todelete"
  case "$mac" in /volume1/mac/Decor/Character\ Licensed/*) ;;
    *) printf 'BAD_MAC_PATH\t%s\n' "$mac"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;;
  esac
  case "$style" in /volume1/styleguides/*) ;;
    *) printf 'BAD_STYLE_PATH\t%s\n' "$style"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue;;
  esac
  if [ -e "$mac" ]; then printf 'MAC_EXISTS\t%s\n' "$mac"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue; fi
  if [ ! -f "$old" ]; then printf 'OLD_MISSING\t%s\n' "$old"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue; fi
  if [ ! -f "$style" ]; then printf 'STYLE_MISSING\t%s\n' "$style"; bad=$((bad+1)); continue; fi
  style_size=$(stat -c '%s' "$style")
  old_size=$(stat -c '%s' "$old")
  if [ "$style_size" != "$expected_size" ] || [ "$old_size" != "$expected_size" ]; then
    printf 'SIZE_MISMATCH\t%s\texpected=%s\tstyle=%s\told=%s\n' "$mac" "$expected_size" "$style_size" "$old_size"
    bad=$((bad+1)); continue
  fi
  style_dev=$(stat -c '%d' "$style")
  parent_dev=$(stat -c '%d' "$(dirname "$mac")")
  if [ "$style_dev" != "$parent_dev" ]; then
    printf 'DIFFERENT_FILESYSTEM\t%s\t%s\n' "$style" "$mac"
    bad=$((bad+1)); continue
  fi
  ready=$((ready+1))
done < "$payload"
printf 'PRECHECK\tready=%s\tbad=%s\n' "$ready" "$bad"
[ "$bad" -eq 0 ] || exit 2
created=0
chmodded=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r mac style expected_size || [ -n "$mac" ]; do
  mac=${mac%$'\r'}; style=${style%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$mac" ] || continue
  ln "$style" "$mac"
  created=$((created+1))
  chmod a-w "$style"
  chmodded=$((chmodded+1))
  style_stat=$(stat -c '%d:%i:%h:%A:%a' "$style")
  mac_stat=$(stat -c '%d:%i:%h:%A:%a' "$mac")
  old_stat=$(stat -c '%d:%i:%h:%A:%a' "${mac}todelete")
  printf 'LINKED\t%s\t%s\tstyle_stat=%s\tmac_stat=%s\told_stat=%s\n' "$style" "$mac" "$style_stat" "$mac_stat" "$old_stat"
done < "$payload"
printf 'SUMMARY\tcreated=%s\tchmodded=%s\n' "$created" "$chmodded"
'@.Replace('\"','"').Replace('\t',"`t").Replace('\n',"`n") | Set-Content -Encoding ascii $script
"$payload`n$script"","workdir":"D:\repos\temp\edgesynology2","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":12000}

payload.message:
I tried the hard-link test, but it aborted before creating anything.

Reason: Synology has `styleguides` and `mac` as separate Btrfs shared-folder mounts/subvolumes. They sit on the same underlying volume, but Linux reports different filesystem device IDs:

```text
styleguides file dev=35
mac folder dev=40
```

Hard links cannot cross that boundary, so `/volume1/styleguides/...` -> `/volume1/mac/...` hard links are not possible.

No files were changed. No chmod/read-only changes were applied. The attempted test folder was:

```text
/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed/____New Structure/Concept Approved Designs/DESKTOP/CDD_Figural ceramic pencil cup/Disney/CDD58DYWP01/For Factory
```

The likely next-best option is a **Btrfs reflink / clone copy** instead of a hard link. That would look like a normal file on Windows/Mac, can be made read-only independently, and may share disk blocks with the styleguide original if Synology allows reflinks across these subvolumes. That’s worth testing next.

    *) printf 'BAD_MAC_PATH\t%s\n' "$mac"; bad=$((bad + 1)); continue ;;
  esac
  case "$style" in /volume1/styleguides/*) ;;
    *) printf 'BAD_STYLE_PATH\t%s\n' "$style"; bad=$((bad + 1)); continue ;;
  esac

  if [ -e "$mac" ]; then
    printf 'MAC_EXISTS\t%s\n' "$mac"
    bad=$((bad + 1))
    continue
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$old" ]; then
    printf 'OLD_MISSING\t%s\n' "$old"
    bad=$((bad + 1))
    continue
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$style" ]; then
    printf 'STYLE_MISSING\t%s\n' "$style"
    bad=$((bad + 1))
    continue
  fi

  style_size=$(stat -c '%s' "$style")
  old_size=$(stat -c '%s' "$old")
  if [ "$style_size" != "$expected_size" ] || [ "$old_size" != "$expected_size" ]; then
    printf 'SIZE_MISMATCH\t%s\texpected=%s\tstyle=%s\told=%s\n' "$mac" "$expected_size" "$style_size" "$old_size"
    bad=$((bad + 1))
    continue
  fi

  ready=$((ready + 1))
done < "$payload"

printf 'PRECHECK\tready=%s\tbad=%s\n' "$ready" "$bad"
[ "$bad" -eq 0 ] || exit 2

created=0
chmodded=0

while IFS=$'\t' read -r mac style expected_size || [ -n "$mac" ]; do
  mac=${mac%$'\r'}
  style=${style%$'\r'}
  [ -n "$mac" ] || continue

  cp --reflink=always "$style" "$mac"
  created=$((created + 1))

  chmod a-w "$mac"
  chmodded=$((chmodded + 1))

  style_stat=$(stat -c '%d:%i:%h:%A:%a:%s' "$style")
  mac_stat=$(stat -c '%d:%i:%h:%A:%a:%s' "$mac")
  old_stat=$(stat -c '%d:%i:%h:%A:%a:%s' "${mac}todelete")
  style_hash=$(sha256sum "$style" | awk '{print $1}')
  mac_hash=$(sha256sum "$mac" | awk '{print $1}')
  old_hash=$(sha256sum "${mac}todelete" | awk '{print $1}')

  printf 'REFLINKED\t%s\t%s\tstyle_stat=%s\tmac_stat=%s\told_stat=%s\tstyle_hash=%s\tmac_hash=%s\told_hash=%s\n' \
    "$style" "$mac" "$style_stat" "$mac_stat" "$old_stat" "$style_hash" "$mac_hash" "$old_hash"
done < "$payload"

printf 'SUMMARY\tcreated=%s\tchmodded=%s\n' "$created" "$chmodded"

payload.message:
Test worked.

Hard links were impossible across the Synology shared-folder boundary, but **Btrfs reflink clone copies do work**. I created five normal-looking reflinked files in the test folder, all cloned from `/volume1/styleguides/...`, and made the mac-side copies read-only.

Go test here:

```text
\\edgesynology1\mac\Decor\Character Licensed\____New Structure\Concept Approved Designs\DESKTOP\CDD_Figural ceramic pencil cup\Disney\CDD58DYWP01\For Factory
```

What you’ll see:

```text
CPNA012064 pooh.ai              <- new reflink copy, read-only
CPNA012064 pooh.aitodelete      <- old duplicate copy, kept for rollback

Pooh 3d Back.png                <- new reflink copy, read-only
Pooh 3d Back.pngtodelete        <- old duplicate copy

Pooh 3d front.png               <- new reflink copy, read-only
Pooh 3d front.pngtodelete       <- old duplicate copy

Pooh 3d side2.png               <- new reflink copy, read-only
Pooh 3d side2.pngtodelete       <- old duplicate copy

Pooh3d side.png                 <- new reflink copy, read-only
Pooh3d side.pngtodelete         <- old duplicate copy
```

Verification from the NAS:

```text
created=5
chmodded=5
hashes match styleguide originals
mac-side files are mode -r-xr-xr-x
styleguide originals remained mode -rwxrwxrwx
```

So this gives us normal files for Windows/Mac users, likely shared disk blocks via Btrfs reflink, and independent read-only permissions on the mac-side copies.

content.text: so you're telling me 'Pooh 3d front.png' shows a size of 64KB, but that's not 64KB here, that's 64KB only in 'styleguides'?

payload.message: so you're telling me 'Pooh 3d front.png' shows a size of 64KB, but that's not 64KB here, that's 64KB only in 'styleguides'?

Almost: it will **show as 64 KB in both places**, because the reflink clone is a real file entry with the same logical file size.

But it should **not consume another full 64 KB of physical disk space** at the moment it is created. With Btrfs reflinks, both files share the same underlying disk extents until one copy is modified. That’s copy-on-write.

So:

```text
/styleguides/.../Pooh 3d front.png       logical size: 64 KB
/mac/.../Pooh 3d front.png               logical size: 64 KB
```

But storage usage should be closer to:

```text
one shared 64 KB-ish data payload
two filesystem entries/metadata records
```

If someone modifies the mac-side copy, Btrfs would allocate new blocks for the changed data. Since we made the mac-side copy read-only, that should be prevented for normal users.

The old file is still there too:

```text
Pooh 3d front.pngtodelete
```

That one is the original duplicate copy and still consumes its own space until deleted.

content.text: and how can this all go wrong and create a mess on my server?

payload.message: and how can this all go wrong and create a mess on my server?

Ways this can go wrong:

1. **Someone can still edit through permissions/ACL loopholes**
   - `chmod a-w` may not fully override Synology ACLs or admin-level access.
   - If a user can write, Btrfs copy-on-write creates new blocks and you lose the storage benefit for that file.

2. **Apps may replace instead of edit**
   - Some Adobe apps save by writing a temp file, deleting/renaming the original, then moving the temp into place.
   - Even if the original file is read-only, behavior can vary depending on folder permissions. You could end up with a new writable full copy, or a save failure, or extra temp files.

3. **The `.todelete` backups create clutter**
   - During testing, every restored visible file has a matching `file.exttodelete`.
   - Until we delete those, the folder is noisier and still uses the old duplicate space.

4. **Reflinks are invisible to users**
   - Windows/Mac users see normal files. That’s good, but also means nobody can tell by looking which files are reflinked styleguide clones versus real working files.

5. **Future edits may silently break sharing**
   - If someone with permission modifies a reflinked copy, it becomes physically separate for changed blocks. It still looks fine, but the storage savings shrink.

6. **Wrong match = wrong replacement**
   - If our duplicate detection were wrong, we could replace a project-specific file with the styleguide original. We reduced this risk by matching filename + size + SHA-256, so this is very unlikely.

7. **Permissions might annoy designers**
   - If they expect to tweak the local copy, they’ll get denied or weird app behavior. That may be exactly what you want for styleguide assets, but it will surface as “file is locked” complaints.

8. **Bulk renames/reflinks can leave partial state**
   - If a script is interrupted, some folders may have:
     - `file.ext` as reflink read-only
     - `file.exttodelete` as old duplicate
     - other files still only as `file.exttodelete`
   - This is recoverable but messy without logs.

9. **Backups/sync/indexing may treat reflinks differently**
   - Some backup tools preserve reflinks efficiently; others read them as full files and back them up as duplicates.
   - Synology snapshots should handle Btrfs sharing well, but external backups may not.

10. **Deleting the styleguide original later**
   - The mac reflink copy remains as its own file if the styleguide original is deleted, because it is not a shortcut. That’s safer than a broken link, but it means governance of “source of truth” can drift.

My practical recommendation: use this pattern, but only with guardrails:

```text
- Keep a TSV log of every replacement.
- Process one top-level area at a time.
- Leave .todelete backups for a short review window.
- Then delete .todelete files after spot checks.
- Use Synology ACLs, not only chmod, if we truly need read-only behavior for designers.
- Do not apply it to active work-in-progress folders until tested with Adobe/Finder/Explorer behavior.
```

The biggest real risk is not data loss; it’s **folder clutter plus confusing read-only behavior** if users try to edit these files.

content.text:
Ways this can go wrong:

1. **Someone can still edit through permissions/ACL loopholes**
   - `chmod a-w` may not fully override Synology ACLs or admin-level access.
   - If a user can write, Btrfs copy-on-write creates new blocks and you lose the storage benefit for that file.

2. **Apps may replace instead of edit**
   - Some Adobe apps save by writing a temp file, deleting/renaming the original, then moving the temp into place.
   - Even if the original file is read-only, behavior can vary depending on folder permissions. You could end up with a new writable full copy, or a save failure, or extra temp files.

3. **The `.todelete` backups create clutter**
   - During testing, every restored visible file has a matching `file.exttodelete`.
   - Until we delete those, the folder is noisier and still uses the old duplicate space.

4. **Reflinks are invisible to users**
   - Windows/Mac users see normal files. That’s good, but also means nobody can tell by looking which files are reflinked styleguide clones versus real working files.

5. **Future edits may silently break sharing**
   - If someone with permission modifies a reflinked copy, it becomes physically separate for changed blocks. It still looks fine, but the storage savings shrink.

6. **Wrong match = wrong replacement**
   - If our duplicate detection were wrong, we could replace a project-specific file with the styleguide original. We reduced this risk by matching filename + size + SHA-256, so this is very unlikely.

7. **Permissions might annoy designers**
   - If they expect to tweak the local copy, they’ll get denied or weird app behavior. That may be exactly what you want for styleguide assets, but it will surface as “file is locked” complaints.

8. **Bulk renames/reflinks can leave partial state**
   - If a script is interrupted, some folders may have:
     - `file.ext` as reflink read-only
     - `file.exttodelete` as old duplicate
     - other files still only as `file.exttodelete`
   - This is recoverable but messy without logs.

9. **Backups/sync/indexing may treat reflinks differently**
   - Some backup tools preserve reflinks efficiently; others read them as full files and back them up as duplicates.
   - Synology snapshots should handle Btrfs sharing well, but external backups may not.

10. **Deleting the styleguide original later**
   - The mac reflink copy remains as its own file if the styleguide original is deleted, because it is not a shortcut. That’s safer than a broken link, but it means governance of “source of truth” can drift.

My practical recommendation: use this pattern, but only with guardrails:

```text
- Keep a TSV log of every replacement.
- Process one top-level area at a time.
- Leave .todelete backups for a short review window.
- Then delete .todelete files after spot checks.
- Use Synology ACLs, not only chmod, if we truly need read-only behavior for designers.
- Do not apply it to active work-in-progress folders until tested with Adobe/Finder/Explorer behavior.
```

The biggest real risk is not data loss; it’s **folder clutter plus confusing read-only behavior** if users try to edit these files.
